pattern: Sort points, then for each pair (A, B) where A is upper-left of B, verify no other point lies strictly inside or on the boundary of their rectangle using a brute-force O(n^3) check.
hints: Point A is upper-left of B when A.x <= B.x and A.y >= B.y (and not both equal); the rectangle they define has corners at A and B.
For a pair (A, B) to be valid, no other point C can satisfy A.x <= C.x <= B.x and B.y <= C.y <= A.y simultaneously.
With n <= 50, an O(n^3) triple-loop checking all pairs and all potential interior points is fast enough.

edge_inputs.description: three collinear points on diagonal, no valid upper-left pair
L-shaped points with blocking point on boundary
two points, A strictly upper-left of B
two points, B is upper-left of A (0 pairs)
point on the rectangle border invalidates the pair
same x or same y (forms a line, still valid rectangle check)
